title: Browser verification transcript semantics evolved from a flat method list to separated events and command responses

claims.summary: The earlier browser-verification prototype treated navigationStarted, responseCompleted, load, script.evaluate, and captureScreenshot as one flat ordered method transcript.

sources.role: correction commit preserving the prior modeling error in its change history

claims.review_route: Inspect the parent and corrected forms of tools/frontier-research/browser-verification-transcript.mjs at commit edec5a1a11ae084e7670008c89061891088c7ce3.

claims.summary: The corrected contract preserves ordered browser events separately from command success or error responses correlated by command id, and hashes decoded screenshot bytes rather than the Base64 transport text.
claims.review_route: Run the seven deterministic contract cases and compare the implementation against current WebDriver BiDi message semantics.

claims.summary: Any Cloudflare or Vercel adapter still emitting script.evaluate or captureScreenshot as synthetic events is incompatible with the corrected v2 evidence contract.
claims.review_route: Inspect downstream adapters and falsify this inference if they already preserve raw event and command-response records with stable command ids.

claims.summary: No retained live-browser transcript yet proves that a current browser implementation normalizes losslessly into the corrected v2 model.
claims.review_route: Resolve only with retained raw messages from a current BiDi-capable browser and a successful v2 normalization result.
